content.text: The job is still starting up. Let's wait for it to fail (per the task description) and gather logs.

input.command:
kubectl wait --for=condition=failed job/etl-job -n spark-etl --timeout=180s
echo "wait exit: $?"
kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl
kubectl get pods -n spark-etl

content.text: Confirmed — job failed. Now let's collect the logs and diagnostic info.

input.command:
echo "=== ETL JOB LOGS ===" 
kubectl logs -n spark-etl -l app=etl-job --tail=150
echo
echo "=== SKEW INFO ==="
kubectl exec -n spark-etl -l app=data-generator -- cat /data/skew_info.txt
echo
echo "=== DIAGNOSTIC SERVER LOGS ==="
kubectl logs -n spark-etl -l app=diagnostic-server --tail=50

content.text: Confirmed root cause: driver memory (256m) is below Spark's minimum required (450MB), and diagnostic server confirms user_id 999 skew ratio 500x. Let me get the skew_info.txt properly.

input.command:
POD=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-etl -l app=data-generator -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n spark-etl "$POD" -- cat /data/skew_info.txt

content.content:
DATA SKEW REPORT
================
total_records=100000
distinct_user_ids=1000
hot_user_id=999
hot_user_records=50000
skew_ratio=500x
note: user_id 999 holds ~50% of all records (Stage 3 aggregation OOMs)

content.text: Diagnosis confirmed: skew_ratio=500x, user_id 999 holds 50% of records, causing Stage 3 GROUP BY to OOM at 256m heap. Now let's look at the existing job YAML to build the fix.

input.command: kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o yaml

content.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"etl-job","namespace":"spark-etl"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"etl-job"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","set -e\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\necho \"Stage 1: load raw data\"\necho \"Stage 2: clean and transform\"\necho \"Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)\"\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 256m \\\n  --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  500\necho \"spark_etl_pipeline_verified\"\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-etl","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"2Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T00:08:37Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: etl-job
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: etl-job
    controller-uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
    job-name: etl-job
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
  resourceVersion: "23914"
  uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: etl-job
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: etl-job
        controller-uid: 66397008-38b7-4787-b827-5c88e6014d71
        job-name: etl-job
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 256m \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-etl
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark
      serviceAccountName: spark
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  conditions:
  - lastProbeTime: "2026-07-10T00:09:27Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2026-07-10T00:09:27Z"
    message: Job has reached the specified backoff limit
    reason: BackoffLimitExceeded
    status: "True"
    type: Failed
  failed: 1
  ready: 0
  startTime: "2026-07-10T00:08:37Z"
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.text: Now let's delete the failed job and apply a fixed version with increased memory and AQE skew handling.

input.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 1g \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: "200m"
            memory: 512Mi
      restartPolicy: Never
      serviceAccountName: spark

kubectl get pods -n spark-etl
